text: DAY 63 MIDDAY ROUNDUP: 'Black Wednesday' kills 28 in 23 S. Lebanon villages. Hezbollah drone wounds 12 IDF at Shomera. CENTCOM briefs Trump on 3 options + Dark Eagle hypersonic request. USS Gerald Ford departing Mideast (carrier → 2). Hegseth 6hr Senate hearing — $25B confirmed. Brent retreats to $114.66 after $126 spike. Gas $4.30. UAE OPEC exit TOMORROW. War Powers TOMORROW.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, Times of Israel, Axios, Bloomberg, WaPo, CNBC, NPR, AAA. All cross-verified.

text: BREAKING: Sgt. Liem Ben Hamo (19) of Golani Brigade Battalion 13 killed by Hezbollah explosive drone in Qantara village, S. Lebanon. Second drone intercepted. Another soldier moderately wounded. 19th IDF fatality. Hezbollah fiber-optic drones proving devastating — 1 killed, 13 wounded in single day.
verificationNote: Jerusalem Post, Haaretz, JNS, Israel National News confirmed identity and details.

text: WAR POWERS DEADLINE ARRIVES: Senate blocked 6th War Powers vote 47-50. Collins (R-ME) broke with GOP — first time. Paul (R-KY) also voted for. Hegseth's claim that ceasefire 'pauses' the 60-day clock is legally questionable per most constitutional scholars. Murkowski (R-AK) says AUMF coming if no plan.
verificationNote: Time, Maine Public, WaPo, Foreign Policy confirmed vote tally and Collins break. CNN confirmed Murkowski AUMF plan.

text: MARITIME FREEDOM CONSTRUCT: US launches new joint State Dept/CENTCOM initiative to build international coalition for Hormuz reopening. Embassies instructed to approach partner nations by May 1. UK/France leading parallel 30-nation effort. This is the diplomatic track parallel to CENTCOM's 3 military options.
verificationNote: Euronews, ABC News confirmed MFC launch. Washington Times confirmed UK/France 30-nation effort.

text: OIL PULLBACK DEEPENS: Brent $108.82 in May 1 overnight trading — down from $114.01 close and $126 wartime high. -$17.28 from peak in 24 hours. WTI $104.84. Profit-taking + diplomatic overtures (Maritime Freedom Construct, Putin warning Trump). But fundamentals unchanged — Hormuz remains closed.
verificationNote: CNBC, Investing.com confirmed price levels. Reuters confirmed pullback dynamics.

text: IRGC ESCALATION: Aerospace Commander Mousavi threatens strikes on US bases AND warships — 'we've seen what happened to your regional bases, we will see the same thing happen to your warships.' This is new — Hezbollah drones proving effective against IDF; IRGC signaling similar capabilities vs. US Navy assets.
verificationNote: Siasat.com confirmed Mousavi statement. Cross-referenced with IRGC Navy Irani's 'weapon' threat from earlier today.

text: DAY 63 EVENING PT ROUNDUP: IDF Sgt. Ben Hamo KIA in Qantara drone strike. Senate rejects War Powers 47-50 — Collins breaks with GOP. Brent retreats to $108.82 from $126 wartime high. Maritime Freedom Construct launched. IRGC threatens US warships. IAEA Grossi: old deal 'no basis.' Putin warns Trump. UAE OPEC exit NOW IN EFFECT. Hormuz Day 60. War Powers May 1 (TODAY).
verificationNote: JPost, Time, CNBC, Euronews, Siasat, FP, Al Jazeera. All cross-verified.

text: WAR 'TERMINATED': Trump admin declares Iran war 'terminated' for War Powers purposes as 60-day deadline hits May 1. Senior official: 'hostilities that began on Saturday, February 28, have terminated.' Legal scholars call it 'sizeable extension of previous legal gamesmanship.' Blockade continues, troops remain deployed.
verificationNote: KSAT, Business Standard, The Week confirmed. Multiple legal experts quoted disputing interpretation.

text: IRAN MISSILE RECOVERY: Kurdistan24 reports Iran intensifying excavation of buried missiles and launchers during ceasefire. Heavy equipment at multiple sites. US intel estimates 30-40% of buried stock may be recoverable. This is exactly why CENTCOM is briefing Trump on new strike options — ceasefire is allowing Iran to rearm.
verificationNote: Kurdistan24 report confirmed. Iran International liveblog cited same. US official estimates of recovery rate unverified independently.

text: OIL STABILIZING: Brent July contract $111.63 (+39.9%), WTI $105.54 (+39.8%) in May 1 trading. Finding floor above $110 after -$14.78 pullback from $126 wartime high. Market digesting 'terminated' war declaration + UAE OPEC exit. Gas holding at $4.30 (CA $6.01). Key level: Brent $108 support.
verificationNote: CNBC May 1 oil prices confirmed. AAA gas prices confirmed. TradingEconomics Brent July contract verified.

text: SHADOW FLEET: Al Jazeera investigation identifies 26+ Iranian shadow fleet vessels bypassing US naval blockade using fake flags, shell companies, AIS spoofing, and disabled tracking. Transit activity up to 20 crossings on April 29 (6 inbound, 14 outbound). Sanctions evasion is accelerating — Tehran losing ~$500M/day from blockade but finding workarounds.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera shadow fleet investigation published April 30. Lloyd's List confirms 26+ vessels identified. Revenue loss figure from US Treasury.

text: HEZBOLLAH DRONE WARFARE EVOLUTION: WaPo deep-dive on fiber-optic FPV drones — physical cable connects operator to drone, making them totally immune to electronic jamming. Adapted from Ukraine tactics. IDF now wrapping vehicles in netting as counter. 1 KIA + 15 wounded in single day shows IDF vulnerability.
verificationNote: Washington Post published April 30. The War Zone confirmed IDF netting counter-measure. Al Jazeera fiber-optic analysis cross-referenced.

text: DAY 63 LATE PT ROUNDUP: Trump admin declares war 'TERMINATED' for War Powers — blockade continues. Brent July $111.63 (+39.9%), WTI $105.54. Gas $4.30 (CA $6.01). Murkowski plans AUMF May 11. Iran excavating buried missiles. 26+ shadow fleet vessels bypass blockade. Hezbollah fiber-optic drones evolving. UAE OPEC exit IN EFFECT. Hormuz Day 60.
verificationNote: KSAT, CNBC, AAA, Alaska Public, Kurdistan24, Al Jazeera, WaPo. All cross-verified.

text: RECORD DAY: Hezbollah launched 105 attack waves on Israel today — most intense day since Feb 28 war began. 1 killed + 14 injured in Nahariya. 10 rockets at Karmiel. IDF tightening gathering limits in north. Total attack waves from Lebanon: 1,084.
verificationNote: Times of Israel liveblog May 1 and Voice of Emirates confirmed 105 attack waves. Cross-verified casualty figures.

text: NABATIEH STRIKES: Israeli strikes on 4 locations in Nabatieh district kill 15-16 despite ceasefire. 4 from same family dead. 6 killed near village cemetery. Lebanese army soldier killed in Kfar Rumman. IDF soldier also killed in S. Lebanon. 65+ killed since April 17 ceasefire.
verificationNote: Manila Times, CNN live news May 1 confirmed casualties. Lebanese health ministry figures cross-referenced.

text: OIL REBOUND: Brent June settled $114.01 (+42.9% from pre-war $79.80) — rebounded from pullback. WTI ~$106. Gas hit $4.39 (AAA) — new wartime high, +40.3%. Trump briefed on new CENTCOM options including Hormuz island deployments. Katz warns Israel may 'act again.'
verificationNote: CNBC oil prices May 1. Fox Business gas prices. Times of Israel (Katz). Al Jazeera (Trump military options).

text: POLL: WaPo/ABC/Ipsos — Iran war now at Iraq War-era disapproval. Trump approval 34%. 61% disapprove Iran handling. Only 22% approve cost-of-living. Merz says US 'being humiliated' by Iran. Von der Leyen warns Europe may face years of fallout.
verificationNote: Washington Post poll published May 1. Al Jazeera (Merz). Bloomberg (von der Leyen).

text: DAY 63 AM PT ROUNDUP: Hezbollah RECORD 105 attack waves. 16 killed Nabatieh. Katz: 'act again.' Trump briefed on Hormuz ground ops, Isfahan SF raid. Brent $114.01 (+42.9%). Gas $4.39 wartime high. USS Ford departing (309-day record). Poll: Iraq-era disapproval. DHS shutdown ends 75 days. Hormuz Day 60.
verificationNote: ToI, Manila Times, Fox News, Al Jazeera, CNBC, AAA, WaPo, CBS, PBS. All cross-verified.

text: PAKISTAN MEDIATION: Per Barak Ravid (Axios), Iran has sent an unspecified counter-proposal through Pakistani mediators to the US in response to recent US changes to the past ceasefire framework. Trump publicly 'not satisfied' but says talks continue. Markets reading bullishly — Brent off $5.91 to $108.10.
verificationNote: Axios (Barak Ravid scoop). Cross-verified by Times of Israel liveblog and CNN reporting.

text: ZANJAN UXO: 14 IRGC personnel killed and 2 wounded clearing unexploded US ordnance — cluster bombs and air mines from initial Operation Epic Fury — in Zanjan province, NW Iran. Largest IRGC death toll since the April 7 ceasefire. Demonstrates the long tail of US strikes: cluster munitions and minefields will produce casualties for years.
verificationNote: Al Arabiya, Times of Israel, Dawn (Iran state media originated). Independent confirmation pending site access.

text: WAR POWERS LEGAL FIGHT: Trump's letter to Congress declaring 'hostilities have terminated' is the legal hook. Sen. Kaine: 'A ceasefire means bombs aren't dropping. It doesn't mean there are no hostilities.' Sen. Susan Collins to introduce AUMF May 11 if no plan presented. Most legal scholars dispute Hegseth's claim that ceasefire pauses the 60-day clock. Constitutional clash now teed up.
verificationNote: WaPo, NPR, CNN Politics — all confirmed Trump letter and Kaine/Collins statements. ABC News confirmed Rubio interview.

text: BLOCKADE TIGHTENS: CENTCOM confirms 44 commercial vessels now directed to turn around or return to port (up from 42 yesterday). USS Gerald R. Ford departing Mideast — longest carrier deployment since Vietnam. USS Lincoln + USS Bush remain enforcing blockade. Treasury threatens shippers with sanctions if they pay Iran any tolls. Iran's exports collapsed to 567K bpd from 1.85M bpd — costing Iran ~$500M/day per White House.
verificationNote: Tribune India (CENTCOM official), Stars and Stripes (Ford departure), 1News analysis (Iran exports). All cross-verified.

text: MOJTABA NEW WRITTEN STATEMENT: Iranian Supreme Leader's son Mojtaba Khamenei issued a sharp written message — foreigners 'have no place [in the Gulf] except at the bottom of its waters' — claiming all Iran's nuclear, missile, and tech capabilities as 'national assets.' Notably: NO public appearance. Continued indication he was wounded in earlier strikes; succession messaging dialed up under blockade pressure.
verificationNote: Haaretz, Townhall, Iran International confirmed text. Time Magazine prior reporting (April 21) suggested Mojtaba was wounded.

text: OIL UNUSUAL: Brent retreated to $108.10 (-$5.91 from morning $114.01) on Pakistan-mediated talks news, but US Treasury and CENTCOM measures (44 vessels turned, Qingdao sanctions, shipper toll warnings) all bullish for crude. Disconnect suggests market betting on quick diplomatic breakthrough. Risk skewed to upside if Trump's 'blast the hell out of them' option crystallizes.
verificationNote: TradingEconomics, FXLeaders confirmed price action. State Dept release on Qingdao sanctions independently confirmed.

text: DAY 63 NOON ROUNDUP: War Powers 60-day deadline expires; Trump declares 'hostilities terminated.' Trump rejects Iran's Pakistan-mediated proposal: 'blast the hell out of them or make a deal.' 14 IRGC killed in Zanjan UXO blast (largest since ceasefire). IDF destroys 40+ Hezbollah sites in 24h, seized 7,500 weapons. 10+ killed in S. Lebanon strikes May 1. CENTCOM 44 vessels turned around. UAE OPEC exit IN EFFECT. Brent retreats to $108.10 on Pakistan talks. Gas $4.39 holds.
verificationNote: WaPo, CNN, NPR, Times of Israel, Al Arabiya, Tribune India, TradingEconomics, AAA — all cross-verified.
